steps.title: Isolate x squared
steps.content:
Start with $2x^2 - 4 = x^2$. Subtract $x^2$ from both sides:
$$x^2 - 4 = 0 \Rightarrow x^2 = 4$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Take the square root of both sides:
$$x = \pm 2$$
So the solutions are $x = 2$ and $x = -2$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $x = 1$ — $2(1)^2 - 4 = -2$, but $1^2 = 1$. Not equal. Eliminate.
  • Choice B: $x = 2$ — $2(2)^2 - 4 = 4$, and $2^2 = 4$. Equal, so $x = 2$ works.
  • Choice C: $x = 3$ — $2(3)^2 - 4 = 14$, but $3^2 = 9$. Not equal. Eliminate.
  • Choice D: $x = 4$ — $2(4)^2 - 4 = 28$, but $4^2 = 16$. Not equal. Eliminate.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
